name: SLB N.V.

profile.description: SLB N.V. (formerly Schlumberger) is the world's largest oilfield services and energy technology company, operating through four divisions: Digital & Integration, Reservoir Performance, Well Construction, and Production Systems. The company provides technology for hydrocarbon production, carbon management, and digital solutions across 100+ countries. Rebranded in 2022 to signal its evolution from oilfield services to energy innovation.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): SLB executed a transformative rebrand from 'Schlumberger oilfield services' to 'SLB energy technology' in 2022, acquired ChampionX for production chemistry, and built a digital subscription business generating $2.4B annually at 32.7% margins — fundamentally reshaping the company's revenue model [1][2]. AI Deployment (2/2): The Delfi cognitive E&P environment is used by 85 of the top 100 global oil producers, leveraging AI to reduce drilling costs and improve reservoir recovery rates; CEO Le Peuch has described AI as 'the X factor for our industry' [2]. Market Share (2/2): SLB is the world's largest oilfield services company by revenue ($35.9B TTM), holding first or second competitive positions in core service lines globally, with Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B up 3% YoY [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): ChampionX integration expected to deliver ~$1.8B incremental revenue and $400M in total synergies; New Energy division (carbon capture, geothermal, critical minerals) scaling toward $10B revenue target by 2040 [1]. R&D Intensity (1/1): SLB's technology R&D investment leads the oilfield services sector, with the Delfi platform, Methane LiDAR Camera (EPA-approved), and next-gen drilling systems under active development [2][3].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): SLB's purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' — is specific, thoughtful, and clearly connects the company's work to a larger purpose beyond profit; the 2022 rebrand validated this purpose by deliberately abandoning the world-famous 'Schlumberger' identity in service of the energy transition vision [2]. Community Impact (1/2): SLB earned a place on the Dow Jones Best-in-Class Sustainability Indices for World and North America, and the EPA-approved Methane LiDAR Camera demonstrates environmental technology investment, but specific charitable foundation dollars or community program amounts are not prominently cited [3]. Employee Retention (1/2): CEO Olivier Le Peuch has served since 2019 after decades at the company, and SLB's 109,000-person multinational workforce across 100+ countries demonstrates organizational stability; the rebrand was managed without major cultural disruption [2]. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful net insider buying by CEO Le Peuch or other executives; no notable open-market purchases identified in recent filings.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on dividend-sleeve metrics. Q1 2026 delivered revenue of $8.72B (up 3% YoY) with adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.1% and GAAP EPS of $0.50 [1]. D/E of 42.5% is well below the 70% target (score 1.0). ROE of 14.1% is near the 15% target (score 0.94). Revenue growth YoY of 2.7% is below the 10% target (score 0.76). Revenue 3-year CAGR of 8.3% is below the 10% target (score 0.89). Forward P/E of 16.3 is below the 20 target (score 1.0). Profit margin of 9.3% is below the 20% target (score 0.46). Payout ratio of 50.7% is above the 40% target (score 0.82). Consecutive years paid: 40+ (score 1.0, 2x weight). Consecutive years growth: 5 (score 0.50). 5yr dividend growth CAGR: 5.4% (score 0.90, 2x weight). Liquidity of $911M daily traded value passes the $1M floor.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (8/9): SLB's core business — providing specialized subsurface imaging, drilling technology, well construction, and production systems — requires deep physical-world expertise, proprietary tools, and on-site operations that AI cannot substitute [1][2]. No AI model can physically drill wells, manage reservoir pressure, or deploy production equipment. SLB is additionally an AI infrastructure provider for the energy industry — the Delfi platform used by 85 of the top 100 global producers is essentially AI-as-a-service for E&P operations, making SLB a beneficiary rather than a victim of AI adoption. AI data center proliferation also increases energy demand, supporting upstream spending. AI Opportunity (1/1): SLB generates meaningful AI revenue through its Digital & Integration division at $2.4B annual run rate with 32.7% margins, including AI-powered tools for drilling optimization, reservoir characterization, and operational decision-making [2].

infinite_game.summary: SLB's 2022 rebrand from 'Schlumberger' to 'SLB' — voluntarily abandoning one of the most recognized names in energy services in service of a broader energy technology vision — is a strong signal of existential flexibility. CEO Le Peuch's investment in carbon capture, geothermal, and digital AI platforms before proven commercial returns demonstrates long-term thinking. However, the transformation remains incomplete, with traditional fossil fuel services still dominating revenue.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): SLB's purpose — 'driving energy innovation for a balanced planet' — is genuine and specific, validated by the transformative rebrand, the $2.4B digital business, and investments in New Energy (carbon capture, geothermal, critical minerals) before proven commercial returns [2]. Strategic decisions — from the rebrand to ChampionX acquisition to Methane LiDAR development — clearly trace back to this purpose. However, the Just Cause has not yet been tested through multi-decade consistency or a leadership transition under the new identity.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): SLB's 109,000-person multinational workforce across 100+ countries represents one of the most diverse workforces in the industrial sector [2]. CEO Le Peuch's multi-decade tenure at the company (promoted from within) and the smooth execution of the 2022 rebrand without major cultural disruption suggest adequate organizational trust. However, oilfield services inherently involves cyclical hiring and the Palliser divestiture signals ongoing portfolio reshaping.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): SLB explicitly benchmarks against Halliburton, Baker Hughes, and emerging technology companies, recognizing that the digital and AI pivot was partly motivated by the threat that tech companies would disrupt traditional field services [2]. The competitive language focuses on evolving SLB's own capabilities rather than defeating specific competitors.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The 2022 rebrand from 'Schlumberger' — a name synonymous with oilfield services for nearly a century — to 'SLB' as a 'global energy technology company' is a textbook Sinek example of existential flexibility [2]. The company is deliberately transitioning from equipment rentals and field manpower to AI-led digital subscription revenue sources, building carbon capture and geothermal businesses before market validation, and acquired ChampionX to reshape its production chemistry portfolio. This is transformation driven by purpose, not desperation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Investing in New Energy businesses (carbon capture, geothermal, critical minerals) before proven commercial returns, rebranding away from the world-famous 'Schlumberger' identity, and building AI platforms for an industry slow to adopt digital tools all required institutional courage [1][2]. However, the core fossil fuel services business still generates the vast majority of revenue and cash flow, suggesting courage is bounded by financial pragmatism rather than representing a full commitment to transformation.

investment_thesis: SLB delivered Q1 2026 revenue of $8.72B (up 3% YoY) with adjusted EBITDA margin of 23.1%, and guided full-year 2026 revenue of $36.9-$37.7B with adjusted EBITDA of $8.6-$9.1B including ~$1.8B of incremental ChampionX revenue [1]. The Digital & Integration division at $2.4B annual run rate with 32.7% margins represents a structurally higher-quality business model than traditional field services. At a forward P/E of 16.3x with a 2.16% dividend yield and the ALIGNED faith alignment score (51), SLB offers a compelling combination of value, income, and values alignment for IOWN investors. The Dow Jones Sustainability Indices membership and MSCI AA ESG rating validate the company's transition toward a 'balanced planet' vision [3].
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on cyclical exposure and transformation timeline: Q1 2026 EBITDA margins declined sequentially due to the Palliser divestiture and Middle East disruptions, and EPS of $0.50 was down from prior-year levels [1]. Revenue growth of 2.7% YoY is modest, and the 3-year revenue CAGR of 8.3% masks a flat 2025 (-1.6% organic). The PEG ratio of 1.89 suggests the stock is not cheap relative to near-term earnings growth. Profit margin of 9.3% remains structurally low for an energy technology company, and the payout ratio of 50.7% exceeds the 40% target for dividend sustainability scoring. The digital transformation, while real, has not yet shifted SLB's overall margin profile — traditional field services remain the vast majority of revenue.
key_catalysts: ChampionX integration delivering ~$1.8B incremental revenue and $400M in total synergies, with roughly half realized in 2026 — creating a path to meaningful EPS accretion
Digital & Integration division scaling toward $3B+ annual revenue with 30%+ margins, each incremental dollar valued at a technology multiple (5-7x revenue) versus field services (1-2x)

New Energy division (carbon capture, geothermal, critical minerals) reaching commercial scale, supported by growing policy incentives for decarbonization and the EPA-approved Methane LiDAR Camera creating new regulatory-driven demand
key_risks: Oilfield services cyclicality: sustained oil price below $60 WTI would reduce E&P budgets across SLB's international customer base (75%+ of revenue), compressing earnings by 15-25%
Profit margin of 9.3% and PEG of 1.89 suggest the stock is fully valued at current growth rates — any revenue shortfall against the $36.9-$37.7B guidance would trigger multiple compression

Digital transformation timeline: the Delfi platform and AI tools generate $2.4B annually but represent less than 7% of total revenue — shifting the overall margin profile requires another 5-10 years of scaling
sources: SLB Q1 2026 Earnings Release — Revenue $8.72B, Full-Year Guidance $36.9-$37.7B, April 2026
